mnemonics: LIMITS: Lonely Isolated Members In The Space. For Limit points, think 'Every Neighborhood Is The Same' - points are everywhere around.
keyPoints: A point x is a limit point of a set S if every open ball centered at x contains a point of S different from x itself.
A point x is an isolated point of a set S if there exists an open ball centered at x that contains no other points of S.
A set is closed if and only if it contains all of its limit points.
commonMistakes: Assuming a set must have limit points. A finite set, for example, has no limit points.
Confusing an accumulation point (limit point) with an isolated point. They are mutually exclusive within a set.
Thinking that if a sequence converges to a point, that point must be a limit point of the sequence's set. This is only true if the sequence is not eventually constant.
